titleEn: AgXeed’s VCU brings existing tractors into the autonomous fold
titleZh: AgXeed推出VCU套件：让现有拖拉机加入自动驾驶阵营
summaryZh: AgXeed发布了一款名为VCU（车辆控制单元）的紧凑型改装套件，可通过ISOBUS TIM协议将现有拖拉机接入其TraXwise规划与监控平台，实现高度自动化的田间作业。该套件支持多品牌拖拉机，可预先规划路线和农具操作，包括自动转弯和地头调头，工作速度0-20km/h。主要面向大田作物、蔬菜种植及大型农场，旨在减少人工干预、降低操作失误，并通过单一界面实现全农场实时监控。
featuredReason: 该技术将自动驾驶能力扩展到现有拖拉机，降低了自动化门槛，对智慧畜牧行业中的种植饲料作物、牧场管理等场景具有直接借鉴价值，体现了软件定义农机、多品牌兼容的行业趋势。
contentFull: AgXeed’s VCU brings existing tractors into the autonomous fold 29-06 | Autonomous/semi-autosteering systems | NewsLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrintThe unit connects via ISOBUS TIM to link the tractor’s controls to TraXwise, AgXeed’s own planning and monitoring platform. Photos: AgXeedAgXeed has introduced its Vehicle Control Unit (VCU), a compact retrofit kit that brings existing tractors into the company’s automated ecosystem. The unit connects via ISOBUS TIM to link the tractor’s controls to TraXwise, AgXeed’s own planning and monitoring platform – allowing standard machines to carry out pre-planned operations with little manual input. The VCU has now been added to the Future Farming Autonomous Retrofit Kits Catalogue.According to Arjan Mulder, Chief Commercial Officer at AgXeed, the benefits of the system lie precisely in that software layer. ‘AgXeed’s technological strength lies in TraXwise, the company’s own planning and monitoring platform supplied with the VCU’, he explains.Through TraXwise, a farmer or manager can plan the entire working route in advance – down to the level of individual fields with varying bed dimensions. ‘Our customer guidance pattern allows you to pre-plan everything, even when you have varying dimensions – say five beds of one metre ten, then a three-metre tramline, then five beds again’, Mulder says.‘It might sound like a small detail, but for vegetable growers it has real practical value. By shifting ten centimetres on one side, growers can fit an entire bed in a single pass – and that can all be planned in advance, in winter already, as soon as the field has been measured. The alternative – taking the tractor into the field and measuring everything manually – takes more time and is more prone to error.’ The VCU is aimed at arable farms, vegetable growers and large-scale farming operations.Multi-brand by designOnce mounted, the VCU links the tractor’s controls to TraXwise, allowing standard vehicles to carry out pre-planned operations – including turns and implement use – in a highly automated way. AgXeed positions the VCU’s most distinctive feature as the ability to pre-plan every operation, both route and implements, from a single place. The system is designed to work as one connected, multi-brand platform that ensures consistent execution, reducing operator stress while enabling live monitoring of the whole farm from one interface.Navigation is handled via GPS with RTK correction, with the vehicle following pre-planned guidance lines generated in TraXwise. The VCU supports autonomous forward and reverse manoeuvring, although with trailed implements, reverse manoeuvres still need to be carried out manually. The system can also perform autonomous headland turns in tight spaces, matched to the tractor’s calibrated turning radius; tighter situations can still be handled manually where needed. Autonomous working speed ranges from 0 to 20 km/h.Implement control over ISOBUS TIMImplements are controlled over ISOBUS TIM, with the tractor functioning as the TIM server and the VCU as the client. A TCU for implement monitoring can also be connected to the ISOBUS plug, and standard ISOBUS features on implements remain available throughout. If an implement requires a TIM functionality not supported by the VCU, that function is simply left unused.At its current stage, the VCU functions as a highly automated system in which the driver supervises the surrounding environment. Errors are displayed on the in-cab tablet, and the tractor stops automatically in the event of a connection loss. An RTK fallback function maintains positioning for eight to 10 minutes if satellite signal is lost. Remote supervision is possible via TraXwise, though one operator cannot yet supervise multiple machines simultaneously – tasks can, however, be planned and dispatched to several machines at once.The VCU is aimed at arable farms, vegetable growers and large-scale farming operations. AgXeed says further integration is in development for when the ISOBUS TIM standard is ready for higher levels of autonomy, including Level 4 autonomy, as well as integration of vehicle data not currently transferred over the TIM protocol. The VCU can be moved to another compatible tractor simply by unplugging the cables and removing the unit from its roof-mounted bracket.Easy to transfer between tractorsAgXeed’s main commercially active countries are Germany, the Netherlands, Australia, Ukraine and Austria, with additional unit support in Canada and the USA. Commercial status is currently limited release for 2026, with full commercial rollout planned for 2027.One practical advantage highlighted by AgXeed is transferability: the VCU can be moved to another compatible tractor simply by unplugging the cables and removing the unit from its roof-mounted bracket. Initial installation requires more time, since the navigation antenna must be positioned and the system calibrated for turning radii and antenna placement, and cabling must be routed to the in-cab connector. Once installed, however, the VCU can be quickly removed and relocated – either to another vehicle or for secure storage.The VCU is priced at €12.500 (US $14,238 at the exchange rate of 29 June 2026), including one year of subscriptions covering RTK, data and TraXwise. Annual software and service costs thereafter amount to €1.300 (US $1,481).Join 17,000+ subscribersSubscribe to our newsletter to stay updated about all the need-to-know content in the agricultural sector, two times a week. translationZh:
AgXeed推出了其车辆控制单元（VCU），这是一款紧凑型改装套件，可将现有拖拉机纳入该公司的自动化生态系统。该单元通过ISOBUS TIM连接，将拖拉机的控制与AgXeed自己的规划与监控平台TraXwise相连——使标准机器能够以极少的人工输入执行预先规划的操作。VCU现已加入《未来农业自动驾驶改装套件目录》。

据AgXeed首席商务官Arjan Mulder称，该系统的优势恰恰在于其软件层。他解释说：“AgXeed的技术优势在于TraXwise，这是随VCU提供的公司自有规划与监控平台。”

通过TraXwise，农民或管理者可以提前规划整个作业路线——细化到具有不同畦面尺寸的单个地块。“我们的客户引导模式允许您预先规划一切，即使面对不同尺寸——比如五畦一米一宽，然后一条三米宽的作业道，再五畦，”Mulder说。

“这听起来可能是个小细节，但对蔬菜种植者来说具有实际价值。通过在一侧偏移十厘米，种植者可以在单次作业中覆盖整个畦面——这一切都可以提前规划，甚至在冬季，一旦地块测量完毕即可完成。而替代方案——将拖拉机开进地里手动测量一切——耗时更多且更容易出错。”VCU面向大田农场、蔬菜种植者和大型农业经营主体。

**多品牌设计**

安装后，VCU将拖拉机控制与TraXwise连接，使标准车辆能够以高度自动化的方式执行预先规划的操作——包括转弯和农具使用。AgXeed将VCU最显著的特点定位为能够从单一位置预先规划每项操作，包括路线和农具。该系统设计为一个互联的多品牌平台，确保执行的一致性，减少操作员压力，同时通过一个界面实现整个农场的实时监控。

导航通过带RTK校正的GPS处理，车辆沿TraXwise中生成的预规划引导线行驶。VCU支持自动前进和倒退操作，但对于牵引式农具，倒退操作仍需手动完成。该系统还可以在狭窄空间内自动执行地头转弯，匹配拖拉机校准的转弯半径；在更紧凑的情况下，仍可手动处理。自动作业速度范围为0至20公里/小时。

**通过ISOBUS TIM控制农具**

农具通过ISOBUS TIM控制，
author: René Groeneveld
contentHtml: AgXeed has introduced its Vehicle Control Unit (VCU), a compact retrofit kit that brings existing tractors into the company’s automated ecosystem. The unit connects via ISOBUS TIM to link the tractor’s controls to TraXwise, AgXeed’s own planning and monitoring platform – allowing standard machines to carry out pre-planned operations with little manual input. The VCU has now been added to the Future Farming Autonomous Retrofit Kits Catalogue.
